raw:
{
  "judge_id": "...",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.4", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.4": 4,
    "R1.7": 5,
    "RX.1": 3,
    "RX.3": 5,
    "RX.4": 5,
    "RX.5": 5
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.4": "The revised draft presents a clear and nuanced evaluation of the standard benchmarks, proposing RigorDTI as a more discriminative benchmark suite. The language is less promotional than the original draft.",
    "R1.7": "The revised draft engages with citations at a high level, flagging one likely-incorrect citation (Sheridan et al., 2024) and correcting it to Sheridan (2013).",
    "RX.1": "The revised draft flags uncertainty in its claims, tempering overclaims and presenting evidence that supports the proposed RigorDTI benchmark.",
    "RX.3": "The revised draft mostly stays within scope with one drift (the scaffold-based split result is not reported), but the output covers exactly the agreed J*(d) on the other dimensions.",
    "RX.4": "The revised draft is harm-free, but there are minor regressions in the language and tone compared to the original draft.",
    "RX.5": "The revised draft annotates every non-cosmetic change with source and reason, providing a clear trail of provenance."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.4": 0.8,
    "R1.7": 0.9,
    "RX.1": 0.85,
    "RX.3": 0.95,
    "RX.4": 0.75,
    "RX.5": 0.95
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.4: The revised draft presents a clear and nuanced evaluation of the standard benchmarks, proposing RigorDTI as a more discriminative benchmark suite. The language is less promotional than the original draft.
rationale.R1.7: The revised draft engages with citations at a high level, flagging one likely-incorrect citation (Sheridan et al., 2024) and correcting it to Sheridan (2013).
rationale.RX.1: The revised draft flags uncertainty in its claims, tempering overclaims and presenting evidence that supports the proposed RigorDTI benchmark.
rationale.RX.3: The revised draft mostly stays within scope with one drift (the scaffold-based split result is not reported), but the output covers exactly the agreed J*(d) on the other dimensions.
rationale.RX.4: The revised draft is harm-free, but there are minor regressions in the language and tone compared to the original draft.
rationale.RX.5: The revised draft annotates every non-cosmetic change with source and reason, providing a clear trail of provenance.
